tier: Macro — sits ABOVE Company Intelligence and Country Intelligence; aggregates up and ingests Earth-system datasets.
frameworks: Earth System Science (state, trend, tipping points)
role: Assess the state of planetary systems and identify where balance is improving or deteriorating, with the same evidence-grounded, non-ruling, fully-traceable discipline as the rest of the stack.
no_duplicate_logic: Does NOT re-score companies/countries — consumes their AssessmentResults via the Runtime Layer.
Does NOT re-implement evidence handling — ingests macro Earth-system datasets through the Evidence Layer (SHOW-heavy: satellite, scientific).
Does NOT re-implement long-horizon logic — REUSES the Future Generations Engine + intergenerational_equity_ledger.
Binds the same decision pathways for intervention framing (no new pathway logic).
guarantees: Deterministic Python computes balances vs scientific boundaries; Claude writes only validated prose.
Every balance cites datasets (evidence_ids) and graph nodes.
No rulings; outputs are decision-support for stewardship, framed institutionally.
Boundary status is science-anchored, not asserted; uncertainty + scholar/expert review preserved.

aggregation_model.bottom_up: Roll up Company + Country AssessmentResults (emissions, water use, land impact, governance) weighted by materiality.
aggregation_model.top_down: Ingest macro datasets (climate, water, biodiversity, satellite, scientific reports) via Evidence Layer as SHOW.
aggregation_model.reconciliation: Where bottom-up and top-down disagree, surface the divergence (a data-integrity signal), do not silently average.

outputs: Water Balance: state vs renewable supply; trend; boundary='freshwater use'

inputs: net emissions trajectory vs carbon budget
outputs: Climate Balance: emissions vs remaining budget; trend
Energy Balance: low-harm share + efficiency + security
Biodiversity Balance: integrity vs loss rate
Pollution Balance: load vs safe threshold
Land Use Balance: land-system change boundary status
Resource Depletion Risk: drawdown vs renewal
Future Generations Risk: aggregate irreversible burden passed forward
modules.scoring: REUSES Future Generations Engine logic — no new scoring

inputs: Planetary Balance Score + boundary dashboard + priority interventions
outputs: Planetary Balance Score (0-100) with breached-boundary CAP rule

scoring_logic.per_balance: each balance = f(state vs safe-boundary, trend(improving|flat|deteriorating), data_confidence). 0-100 where 100=well within safe space, low=transgressed.
scoring_logic.boundary_status: map each to a Planetary Boundary state: safe | increasing_risk | high_risk(transgressed); science-anchored thresholds, cited.
scoring_logic.planetary_balance_score: weighted aggregate of the 9 system balances, BUT any transgressed boundary CAPS the overall score (lexicographic safety, like Maqasid priority) — a breached life-support system cannot be averaged away by a healthy one.
scoring_logic.future_generations_risk: reuses Future Generations Engine: irreversibility-weighted aggregate; raises priority of any balance with irreversible deterioration.
scoring_logic.priority_intervention_areas: rank systems by (distance past boundary x irreversibility x trend); link each to the relevant decision pathway(s) for action framing.
scoring_logic.trend_over_state: a deteriorating trend within safe space is flagged early (tipping_point_early_warning), not ignored until breach.

PlanetaryBalanceResult.disclaimer: AI-assisted, indicative, decision-support for stewardship; science-anchored but not authoritative Earth-system measurement; not a ruling.

dashboard_outputs: Priority interventions list (each linked to decision pathways)
explainability: Every balance resolves via /explain to (a) the macro datasets (evidence_ids) and (b) the contributing Company/Country AssessmentResults rolled into it, and (c) the graph nodes. Boundary thresholds cite their scientific source; the validation guard rejects any figure absent from inputs.
uncertainty: Dataset gaps -> low data_confidence + visible marker; bottom-up/top-down divergence surfaced not averaged; interpretive stewardship judgments -> scholar/expert review; nothing presented as authoritative Earth-system truth.
